syllabus.topic: Tutorial Discussion and Review of Prior Material
sub_points: Clarification of tutorial questions (Q1–Q3) and common misconceptions
Reinforcement of core pattern recognition workflow: features → model → decision rule
Interpretation of classification results through examples and geometric intuition

syllabus.topic: Foundations of Linear Classifiers: Discriminant Functions and Decision Geometry
sub_points: Role of linear classifiers as baseline models: simplicity, interpretability, and limits
Discriminant functions and how they induce decision regions/surfaces
Linear discriminant functions and the decision hyperplane representation
Geometric visualization of hyperplanes and classification in low-dimensional examples

syllabus.topic: Perceptron Learning Algorithm: Formulation, Optimization View, and Variants
sub_points: Perceptron as an iterative update rule for learning a separating hyperplane
Optimization perspective: loss/cost formulation and relationship to gradient descent
Step-by-step dynamics via visualizations and worked examples
Implementation details and practical considerations (updates, convergence behavior)
Extensions: algorithmic variants and multi-class generalizations; conditions for linear separability

syllabus.topic: Linear Classification via Least-Squares / MSE Criteria
sub_points: Using mean-square error (MSE) as an alternative objective for classification
Least-squares estimation (LSE): matrix form, solution concepts, and error-squares minimization
Over-determined vs under-determined systems and implications for solvability
Worked example of LSE-based classifier fitting
Capabilities and practical limits of linear classifiers under MSE/LSE training
Definitions of bias and variance and how they relate to model complexity
Trade-off between underfitting and overfitting in supervised learning
Impact of training procedure and hypothesis class on expected generalization error
Motivation for moving beyond linear models when bias is too high

syllabus.topic: Motivation for Nonlinear Classifiers: Limitations of Linear Decision Boundaries
sub_points: Recap of linear classifiers (perceptron, error minimization) as a starting point
Identifying failure cases for linear separability
XOR as a canonical example requiring non-linear decision boundaries
Problem framing for introducing multi-layer and basis-function methods

syllabus.topic: Two-Layer Perceptron and Multi-Layer Perceptrons (MLPs): Representation and Training
sub_points: Two-layer perceptron architecture and how hidden layers enable non-linear separation
Biological inspiration and neuron model components (weights, bias, activation)
Activation function constraints: continuity/differentiability and implications for learning
Training MLPs by optimizing a cost function; motivation for smooth activations
Backpropagation: gradient computation through layers and iterative parameter updates; brief view beyond the basic MLP

syllabus.topic: Radial Basis Function (RBF) Networks: Concepts and Hybrid Training
sub_points: RBF network structure and intuition: localized basis functions for non-linear modeling
Choice and behavior of radial basis functions (e.g., Gaussian-like responses)
Training pipeline as a hybrid process (e.g., selecting centers/widths then fitting output layer)
Example workflow incorporating model selection/validation (e.g., cross-validation) and performance considerations
